purpose: Fixes a potential use-after-free vulnerability in Bluetooth HCI command queuing when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set, preventing invalid memory access after device unregistration.
The commit removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h from n_hdlc.c, as linux/tty.h already includes linux/termios.h. It addresses code cleanup and reduces complexity.

This commit updates documentation for the perf c2c tool to reflect the addition of an 'N/A' metric for store operations in the report table. It is a documentation-only change with no functional code modifications.

Fixes race conditions and synchronization issues in hugetlb vma locking during split vma and range unmapping. Addresses problems with vma lock handling in pmd sharing and ensures correct lock management during vma duplication and unmap operations.
This commit adds documentation for AVDD and MICVDD power supplies used by the rt5682s codec in device tree bindings. It references existing usage in sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi.
Fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver by adding a missing iounmap() call in the remove function to match the error handling path in the probe function.
Removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the Mellanox mlx5e driver. The change is minimal and purely structural, eliminating dead code.

The commit changes the type of the key_idx field in cfg80211_auth_request from unsigned to signed, allowing it to hold the value -1 when a key is NULL. This prevents warnings from static checkers like smatch.
Sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs to match savedefconfig's ordering. No functional changes expected.

The commit introduces a new helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip is in CAN-FD mode, replacing an inline check. It's a refactoring change to improve code clarity and maintainability.
The commit modifies RTC selftests to skip execution when no RTC device is present, preventing test failures in environments lacking RTC hardware.

Removes a redundant variable assignment in ALSA echoaudio driver that was causing a clang scan build warning. The variable 'bytes' is assigned but never read before being reassigned in a subsequent if block.
This commit allows the rcuscale feature to be built without requiring RCU Tasks, reducing kernel size and tracing complexity. It decouples the two components when CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y.
This commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' from spte.h, correcting a typo in the codebase. The change is minimal and affects only a comment or string literal.
Reverts a change that populated DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The revert prevents a no-read situation caused by PMFW behavior when DCFCLK states are disabled, avoiding potential underflow.
Documents support for a new Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with Mali-G31 GPU by adding a compatible string in device tree bindings.

Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for consistency with other naming conventions in the DW PCIe driver. The change aligns the naming scheme with existing patterns using 'dw_' prefix and '_ep' suffix for endpoint-related entities.

Fixes a Clang compiler warning by correcting the format specifier for an unsigned short argument in a printf-like function call. The change ensures proper type handling according to C standard integer promotions.
The commit fixes a branch range check in MIPS jump_label implementation by casting the upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing a warning triggered by negative offsets.

The commit removes a specific case statement HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD from the SetHwReg8188EU() function, calling the underlying function directly instead. It is part of a larger effort to simplify the HAL layer.

The commit declares a non-global symbol as static to fix a sparse warning. It addresses a specific issue where a symbol was not properly declared as static, causing a warning during kernel compilation.
The commit corrects incorrect interrupt flag specifications in MDSS device tree bindings. It removes bogus interrupt flags cells from consumer nodes, as the MDSS interrupt provider is a single cell.
The commit allocates space for arrays in the struct ctrl_ref to prepare for allowing drivers to change the array size. It is a preparatory change for future flexibility in V4L2 controls.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs to the socinfo driver, expanding hardware compatibility and identification capabilities.
The commit updates the rtl8xxxu WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (letters A to P) instead of only up to E, preventing 'unknown' output for newer chip versions.

The commit removes a custom software enable count from PRCMU clocks, relying instead on the core's existing software enable count. It simplifies the clock management logic by eliminating redundant state tracking.

The commit refactors #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage in RISC-V memory management code. It groups related preprocessor directives and moves macro definitions closer to their usage to reduce conditional compilation clutter.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware support in the device tree for the sm6350-lena board, preparing for future I2C node additions. This is a hardware configuration change.
This commit fixes comments in the rzg2l thermal driver, correcting terminology and formatting to improve code clarity and adherence to coding style.
Fixes an infinite loop in the igc driver's release_swfw_sync function when hardware semaphore acquisition fails, typically due to surprise removal. The fix logs an error and exits gracefully.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to use modern DAI format terminology for clocking, replacing older defines. It is a refactoring effort to align with newer kernel coding standards.
Removes redundant code that checked for IS_SWAPFILE macro, which is always defined. The commit cleans up unnecessary conditional logic.

This commit implements GTP echo response functionality in the kernel, ensuring that GTP-U instances respond to echo requests according to the GTP specification. It adds handling for GTP_ECHO_REQ messages and sends appropriate GTP_ECHO_RSP responses with recovery information elements.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, and introduces cpumask_first_zero(). It improves efficiency by using a more effective function when n == -1.

The commit removes a call to the 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in the brcmfmac driver. This call was failing in firmware, causing error messages on console during STA roaming. The change prevents the host driver from attempting the unsupported firmware operation.
Adds support for a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for the sof_rt5682 driver, enabling compatibility with alc5682 headset codec without speaker amplifier, connected via SSP0.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail gracefully when the underlying socket protocol does not support zerocopy. It extends a previous fix to send requests to also cover sendmsg.

The commit modifies the kerneldoc preamble to test for the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing xelatex errors on systems where the package is not available. This addresses a specific issue in RHEL 9 and clones where texlive-xecjk is missing a dependency.

This commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow 'case' macros without requiring parentheses around complex macro values. It addresses a false positive error in the kernel's coding style checking tool.
The commit converts drm/simpledrm to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers, improving code architecture and sharing among drivers. No functional changes are introduced.

Adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel execution of three hardware units in MT8195. This is a new feature to improve decoding performance by utilizing multiple HW engines simultaneously.
Removes unnecessary VPRINTK() calls from libata, as their information is covered by tracepoints. Also removes ata_scsi_dump_cdb() since scsi tracing can provide the same data.

This commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD display drivers. It enables entering zstate when transitioning into FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO, using extra vblank time for optimization. The change involves calculating time gains, checking thresholds, and updating registers accordingly.

Fixes a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure by correcting the type of the reset_type parameter in the hqd_destroy() callback function to match the actual implementation, which uses enum kfd_preempt_type instead of uint32_t.
Corrects the property name 'reset' to 'resets' in device tree bindings for pinctrl STMicroelectronics STM32. Adds 'additionalProperties: false' to prevent future incorrect properties.

This commit silences an array-bounds warning in the ath9k wireless driver when compiled with GCC 12. It relegates the warning to W=1+ level instead of treating it as an error, without changing functional behavior.

The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver. This change simplifies the code by using a more appropriate runtime power management function that combines resume and get operations.

The commit reorganizes io_uring file installation and removal logic by moving it to the filetable code section. It also changes helper functions to accept a context instead of an io_kiocb, improving code structure and clarity.

Splits G1 and G2 decoder nodes in device tree bindings for i.MX8MQ VPU, enabling independent control via power-domain driver. Updates bindings to reflect separate hardware blocks sharing only a power domain.
The commit removes a macro IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON and inlines its content, as it's only used once. This is a minor refactoring change.
